title: pastinaca sativa subsp. opaca
intro: Pastinaca sativa subsp. opaca, also known as the wild parsnip, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible root. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of wild parsnip include its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of certain diseases. Wild parsnip is often used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and teas, although it is essential to note that the plant's sap can cause severe burns and blisters upon contact with the skin, making proper handling and preparation crucial.
